title: MITSUNOBU REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Mitsunobu reaction describing inversion-based substitution of alcohols using phosphine/azodicarboxylate reagents and diverse nucleophiles.
notes: Key concepts: activation of secondary alcohols by phosphine and dialkyl azodicarboxylates, inversion of configuration, broad oxygen/nitrogen/sulfur/carbon nucleophile scope, and mechanistic participation of zwitterionic phosphonium intermediates.

title: MITSUNOBU REACTION
summary: Application page for the Mitsunobu reaction featuring (+)-zampanolide, ent-WIN 64821, (+)-duocarmycin A, and (+)-fascicularin assembled through inversion-enabled alcohol substitution or carbon-bond-forming Mitsunobu variants.
notes: Examples emphasize selective inversion of secondary alcohols, azide introduction, unusual transannular or carbon-bond-forming Mitsunobu cases, and late-stage deployment in complex natural-product synthesis.

title: MIYAURA BORATION
summary: Overview page for Miyaura boration describing palladium-catalyzed conversion of aryl or vinyl halides and triflates into boronic esters using bis(pinacolato)diboron or tetraalkoxydiborons.
notes: Key concepts: Pd-catalyzed borylation of aryl/vinyl electrophiles, use of B2pin2 or related diborons, importance of acetate base, compatibility with cross-coupling workflows, and catalytic-cycle steps of oxidative addition, transmetalation, and reductive elimination.

title: MIYAURA BORATION
summary: Application page for Miyaura boration featuring TMC-95A, a biaryl-containing macrocycle, (+)-spiroxin C, and terbenzimidazole prepared via late-stage installation of boronic ester partners for Suzuki coupling.
notes: Examples emphasize preparation of arylboronic esters for peptide and macrocycle assembly, domino boration/Suzuki sequences, and iterative use of Miyaura boration to access medicinally relevant heterobiaryls.

title: MUKAIYAMA ALDOL REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Mukaiyama aldol reaction describing Lewis-acid-promoted addition of silyl enol ethers to aldehydes or ketones to form beta-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
notes: Key concepts: preformed enol-silane nucleophiles, Lewis acid activation of carbonyl electrophiles, anti/syn stereochemical models, catalytic asymmetric variants, and broad use in fragment coupling for polyketide synthesis.

title: MUKAIYAMA ALDOL REACTION
summary: Application page for the Mukaiyama aldol reaction featuring rutamycin B, sphingofungin B, and roflamycoin synthesized through stereocontrolled aldehyde/enol-silane couplings.
notes: Examples emphasize anti-selective and asymmetric catalytic Mukaiyama aldol couplings for polyketide fragment assembly, including chiral Lewis acid and organocatalytic control of multiple stereocenters.

title: MYERS ASYMMETRIC ALKYLATION
summary: Overview page for the Myers asymmetric alkylation describing highly diastereoselective alpha-alkylation of pseudoephedrine-derived amide enolates to access optically active carboxylic-acid derivatives and related products.
notes: Key concepts: pseudoephedrine auxiliary-controlled alkylation, LiCl-assisted enolate generation, broad alkyl halide scope, high alpha-face selectivity, and downstream conversion of alkylated amides into acids, aldehydes, or alcohols.

title: MYERS ASYMMETRIC ALKYLATION
summary: Application page for the Myers asymmetric alkylation featuring borrelidin, amphidinolide T1, (-)-dictyostatin, and (+)-kalkitoxin assembled through pseudoephedrine-auxiliary-controlled alpha-alkylation.
notes: Examples emphasize installation of remote stereocenters in macrocyclic and lipophilic natural products using auxiliary-directed alkylation followed by hydrolysis, reduction, or oxidation sequences.

title: NAGATA HYDROCYANATION
summary: Overview page for the Nagata hydrocyanation describing conjugate addition of cyanide to activated alkenes or enones using dialkylaluminum cyanides or HCN/AlX3 combinations.
notes: Key concepts: 1,4-addition of cyanide to alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and related acceptors, use of Et2AlCN or HCN/AlEt3, strong solvent and Lewis-acid effects, and hydrolysis to beta-cyano carbonyl compounds.

title: NAGATA HYDROCYANATION
summary: Application page for the Nagata hydrocyanation featuring (+)-scopadulcic acid B, (+)-3a-acetoxy-7,16-seco-trinerva-7,11-dien-15b-ol, and the ABC carbon skeleton of radarins assembled through conjugate cyanide addition to enones.
notes: Examples emphasize hydrocyanation of rigid cyclic and macrocyclic enones, stereocontrol in bicyclic systems, and strategic use of the installed nitrile as a precursor to methyl or carbonyl functionality.
